title: Michi, Deuchi, and Hasaka
description: Three friends spend Valentine's Day together at the mall, reflecting on their friendship and what the future holds.
content:
Hey, so… it is Valentine's Day.

Deuchi glanced at me, then at Michi. You know, it's totally fine if you two wanna ditch me and run off on your own. She even let out this 'heh heh heh' villain laugh. Of course, no one stood up.

Not only that—none of us knew how to react. Because, deep down, we all sort of felt the same way. Yeah. She had a point.

Valentine's Day was right around the corner, and yet we were, as usual, hanging out together at the mall. We bought Frappuccinos, sat down at an empty table in the third-floor food court, and that was that. Everything was so routine, so natural, that none of us questioned it until we were already sitting down.

Michi, Deuchi, and me.

We were just a random trio who happened to fall together after entering our second year. Somehow that flimsy connection had stuck all the way until now. Before we knew it, it was already February. Though honestly, once we hit our third year and get new classes, who knows if this will continue? In that sense… yeah, our friendship is pretty shallow. We only ever hang out to kill time after school anyway.

So like, are you guys even doing anything serious for Valentine's?

Michi glanced around the mostly empty food court. Even though it was almost dusk, the only people around were families. No one was sitting outside — too cold. Through the glass, the sunset was already breaking apart into orange shards across the sky. The clouds looked like they'd been eaten away by something, charred with dark spots everywhere.

I stared blankly at the view and loudly sucked on my straw. I bought the drink because the shop claimed it was some kind of 'local specialty,' though honestly, I don't even like coffee. There was a takoyaki stand nearby, and the smell of sauce sizzling on the griddle drifted over nonstop.

Every scent and flavor blended together through my nose, mouth, and ears until it all turned into a mess.

What does 'taking it seriously' even mean? I asked.

Serious like… you buy chocolate and go all 'kyaa~ wow~' about it.

So basically, they had no concrete idea. Not that I could explain it either… Ah, but there was something I heard once.

Thumb wrestling?

Huh? How is that even related!?

Michi raised an eyebrow. Right. No one else would understand. Ugh, this is all Shimamura's fault for saying weird things—I smacked the shoulder of a certain classmate who wasn't even here.

Do you think anyone in our class actually paired up?

Probably? Maybe?

Both Michi and Deuchi sounded vague and unsure—clearly not confident.

We started naming classmates who seemed suspicious, nodding along. Some I recognized, some I didn't. Rumors were always bouncing around the cramped classroom and hallways. We, as high schoolers, pretended to be cool and indifferent, but in reality we noticed everything. And if you get honest with someone else? You either get embarrassed, or scared… Basically, we complicate everything.

Oh yeah, what about Adachi and Shimamura? The moment their names came up, I froze.

Adachi and Shimamura… they're seriously a thing, right? Michi grinned.

Well, yeah. Anyone would think that. Even though I wasn't involved at all, I somehow felt embarrassed.

Really? For real?

You seriously hadn't noticed?

But maybe they're just close friends, you know?

Saying that, Deuchi dramatically grabbed Michi's arm. Wait—shouldn't she be hugging her instead?

Michi laughed and complained, 'You're heavy!' and pulled away. Deuchi laughed too, insisting, 'I'm not heavy,' though her eyes didn't match her smile.

And just like that, the topic of Adachi and Shimamura drifted off.

I was the only one who'd actually asked them directly before. But for Michi, it didn't matter either way. We immediately moved on.

Like, say I got a boyfriend sometime soon, Michi said, fiddling with her straw.

And this is hypothetical, okay?

Here, I'll make it happen!

I clapped my hands together in a dramatic pose. Forbidden Technique: Boyfriend Summoning.

Congrats, congrats! Deuchi joined in with applause. Meanwhile, Michi kept bending her straw back and forth.

So then, if that happened… and we couldn't hang out like this as often… would you guys be upset?

At that moment, I felt like Michi had quietly stepped into our little territory—just barely, like one foot touching the border.

I exchanged a look with Deuchi.

…Could you even get one?

It's a hypothetical!

Maybe she already had someone in mind? I wondered as I thought it over.

…I'd be upset—well, saying 'upset' sounds too heavy. So more like… yeah, I'd feel bored, kinda lonely, not sure what to do… Ahhh, see? That is upsetting. So don't go! Don't leave us~

This time, I grabbed onto Michi's arm. She yelped, 'Let go!' and tried to shake me off, but unlike when she shrugged off Deuchi, she couldn't escape. Her yells devolved into 'Gyaaahhh!!' as she flailed harder, but she still didn't budge.

Seriously, let go.

Don't leaaave~

I'm not leaving! I'm not going anywhere! There's nowhere to go anyway!

Deuchi chimed in, stumbling over her words: I wouldn't like it either. Because… um… it's fun. Being together.

Honestly, if it were just me and Deuchi, we probably wouldn't hang out much. If it were just me and Michi, same thing. And if it were just Michi and Deuchi? Also the same.

So Deuchi grabbed Michi too, and the three of us ended up in this weird tangled shape.

Once things settled—'Wanna go buy chocolate?' Michi suggested.

Because it's Valentine's?

Yeah.

Eating is faster than gifting, right? Just us three. Not like we had anyone to give it to anyway.

Michi muttered, 'So we're just eating chocolate…?'

Deuchi replied, 'Sounds good to me.'

Chocolate's sweet, after all.

Yeah. Sweet.

Then let's go buy some.

With that, Michi stood up. Deuchi followed, holding her Frappuccino.

As always—the three of us together.

It made me happy. But after today… There's only half of February left, then March.

Even without boyfriends, we probably don't have much time together.

If we reached out, maybe things would change… But would we actually do it? Did I want it enough to try? I kept circling the same questions without finding an answer. Time was running out, yet we stayed where it was safe, unable to move.

Because once you make a little corner of the world feel safe, stepping out of it demands effort, and the courage to face whatever comes next.

But—I propped my chin on my hands and thought quietly. And yet, Adachi and Shimamura chose each other. Despite knowing heartbreak would inevitably come—loneliness, weariness, all of it—they kept walking the same path, guided by a feeling that felt unfamiliar and precious.

Ah… so that's how it is.… I finally understood that.

I thought of the way they looked at each other. And honestly, I thought it was kinda nice.

Hey.

I called out to the two walking ahead. They slowed down, and I hurried to catch up.

Next year… let's still be us, okay?

Because this comfort is ours. Let's stay like this—for as long as time will let us.
translatorNote:
'Michi, Deuchi, and Hasaka' – A Quick Guide to Who's Who
Sancho (三地) = Michi
Delos (出内) = Deuchi
Pancho (羽坂) = Hasaka (the narrator)
Additionally, there was no furigana provided, and their names are quite unusual, so these readings are educated guesses at best.
